description_long: This is a roughly cubic stone block with a weathered, natural surface and a prominent carved relief on one face. The carving depicts a stylized human or mythological figure with a headdress and symmetrical features, suggesting historical or cultural significance. The other sides of the block are unadorned, showing irregular cracks, chips, and natural stone texture. The coloration is predominantly a light, dusty beige with subtle variations and darker veins, typical of aged limestone or sandstone. The block appears solid and heavy, with a coarse, matte finish that suggests exposure to the elements. Its size and decorative face indicate it may have served as a structural or ornamental element in an urban or historical setting.
description: A large, weathered stone block with a detailed carved relief on one face and rough, cracked surfaces on the others. The block is beige with natural stone textures and signs of age.
description_view_0: The front face features a prominent carved relief of a human or mythological figure, surrounded by rough, cracked stone.
description_view_1: This side is unadorned, showing only natural stone texture, cracks, and some surface irregularities.
description_view_2: Another plain side with visible cracks and chips, lacking any carvings or distinct features.
description_view_3: A rough, uncarved face with pronounced cracks and a few angular chips, similar to the other plain sides.
attribute_location_context: ancient temple foundation or historical urban plaza
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on a prepared stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows significant weathering with cracks, chips, and a generally rough texture, indicating long-term exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A detailed carved relief of a human or mythological figure is present on one face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a larger structure or decorative arrangement, possibly aligned with other carved blocks.
